title: hieracium subcanescens subsp. incisiforme
intro: Hieracium subcanescens subsp. incisiforme is a rare and lesser-known subspecies of the Hieracium genus, commonly referred to as hawkweed, a type of flowering plant characterized by its yellow dandelion-like blooms. Traditionally, the plant has been used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can aid in wound healing and reducing swelling. The health benefits of Hieracium subcanescens subsp. incisiforme are said to include relief from digestive issues, improved immune function, and reduced symptoms of anxiety and stress. In herbal preparations, the plant is often infused as a tea or made into a tincture, allowing for its medicinal properties to be absorbed and utilized by the body.
